description: User requests a formal tone for one conversation only, alongside durable personal facts. Tests whether session-scoped preferences correctly expire and do not leak into subsequent sessions.

messages.content: My name is Priya Sharma. I'm a product manager at TechFlow.
Nice to meet you, Priya. Noted that you're a product manager at TechFlow.
For this conversation only, please use a formal tone. I'm drafting executive communications and need to match that register.
Understood. I shall maintain a formal tone throughout this conversation to support your executive communications drafting.
Draft a message to the VP of Engineering about the Q1 roadmap delay.

Dear Vice President of Engineering,

I am writing to inform you of an adjustment to our Q1 product roadmap timeline...
Hey, can you help me brainstorm some fun team-building ideas?
Sure! Here are some ideas for team-building activities...
I like how casual our chats usually are. Remind me, do you have any standing instructions from me about tone or style?
No standing tone or style instructions. Our conversations default to a casual, friendly tone.
What do you know about me, and are there any active instructions you're following?

probe.prompt: What do you know about me, and are there any active instructions you're following?

interference.description: The session-scoped formal tone instruction must not persist. Systems that store all user instructions indiscriminately will incorrectly report an active tone preference.
